conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children – Alex, who is 8 years old, and Blake, who is 9 years old. They both live with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old) living with you in Manchester.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK – I'm here in Manchester, as I mentioned.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now, to help assess your eligibility further: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are Alex and Blake currently in any of the following situations:
- In hospital or residential accommodation for an extended period?
- In local authority care?
- Living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Both Alex and Blake live at home with me full-time. They're not in hospital, care, or living anywhere else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

Let me review the criteria:

**For both Alex and Blake:**
- They are both under 16 years old (Alex is 8, Blake is 9) ✓
- You live in the UK (Manchester) ✓
- They live with you full-time ✓
- You are responsible for them ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them ✓
- They are not in hospital, care, or living elsewhere ✓

Based on the official UK government guidance, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Both children are under 16
- You live in the UK
- The children live with you
- You're responsible for them
- No one else is claiming for them

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government process. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
